text: i've said before that I've always had
misgivings about season 2 of The Last of
Us because like anyone who played the
game I know exactly what it's leading
to And the first episode seemed to bear
out a lot of my concerns A tedious slog
through trit interpersonal dramas and
angsty teen romances Ellie's gay by the
way A poorly explained conflict between
the two leads that will only make sense
if you've played the games a lightweight
action sequence that reduced the
infected to more of a mild inconvenience
than an existential threat to humanity
and possibly one of the most insane
fight scenes ever committed to film So
it was kind of a cautious underwhelming
start to the season that didn't give us
a whole lot to grapple with Episode 2 on
the other hand is where [ __ ] gets real
And boy does it get real fast But how
does it compare to the game you ask Is
it better or worse And can it possibly
make the overall narrative of The Last
of Us Part Two work Well grab your bigot
sandwiches and let's find out together
shall we Now obviously this review is
going to contain all kinds of delicious
spoilers for both the show and the game
And if you don't want to know what's
coming up then go take part in something
more enjoyable like an exorcism Anyway
the episode centers around two major
events a mass attack against the
settlement of Jackson that provides a
neat distraction and forms a kind of
apocalyptic backdrop to the big
character moment of the show which is
the death of Joel at the hands of Abby
Although in honor of Game Abby and the
fact that they clearly couldn't find an
actual human being of uh her proportions
I'm going to refer to her from now on as
Mini Gigantor Anyway the gist is that
Mini Gigantor and her band of not so
merry men have tracked Joel back to his
home in Jackson But because the place is
heavily fortified there's no way they
can fight their way inside to get to him
But what to do Mini Gigantor doesn't
have any [ __ ] ideas So she goes off
to stand on a mountain for a while and
freeze her ar while she pers the
improbability of female bodybuilders in
a post-apocalyptic world without access
to steroids and HGH The irony here
though is that if they just observed the
place for a while they would have
realized that Jackson basically has an
open door policy when it comes to
refugees and they probably could have
bluffed their way inside and killed Joel
at their leisure Luckily for them though
and for the convenience of the plot the
mountain comes to Muhammad in this case
when Joel goes out on patrol and just so
happens to run into Mini Gigantor as
she's being attacked by a horde of the
infecteds
What a stroke of luck Unable to get back
to Jackson the group take shelter in the
lodge where the rest of Mini Gigantor's
group are holed up allowing her to get
the drop on him and savagely beat him to
death just as Ellie arrives to witness
it So far so Last of Us Part Two But how
does the show compare to the game Well
the interesting thing is that it's
actually both better and worse depending
on your perspective here First up I'll
give you the positives One of the
biggest and best changes is the big
setpiece battle in Jackson which never
actually happens in the game but
provides a muchneeded kick in the ars
for this season and a reminder of how
dangerous the infected actually are in
large numbers It's wellshot epic
visceral action-packed and for once the
human defenders actually make smart use
of their resources mostly I'm not sure
about the resolution though I mean once
the infected break into the place in
large numbers it really seems like
they've been overrun But when we cut
back to it at the end of the episode the
defenders are mopping up the last of the
infected with no clear indication of how
the tide turns All this is really just
window dressing though The more
important change is the confrontation
between Joel and Mini Gigantor And this
is where things are going to get
contentious See the showrunners are
clearly aware of the criticism leveled
at the game and the generalized hatred
for Abby as a character where she
basically just shows up and kills Joel
without any explanation Here there's a
much more obvious attempt to develop her
motivations and show a more human
vulnerable side to her right off the bat
The actress does a great job with the
material really selling the idea of a
loving daughter out to avenge the death
of her father And overall I empathized
with her a lot more than I did with the
game version which isn't exactly
difficult I've empathized more with
things floating in my toilet bowl than
that big slab of beef The setup has also
changed slightly so that Joel is paired
up with Dena on patrol now who gives
away his name in the heat of the moment
and tips Mini Gigantor off that she's
found her man Again a small change but
an important one to make Joel less of a
[ __ ] [ __ ] That being said it's still
frustrating as [ __ ] that he doesn't even
attempt to justify his actions from the
first season when he's given ample
opportunities to do it I'm not saying it
would have changed her mind or anything
but it might at least give her pause for
thought if he'd said "I'm really sorry
but your dad was about to murder an
innocent girl that I care deeply about
and I didn't want to kill him but it was
the only way to save her life And if you
were in my position you'd have done
exactly the same thing young lady Oh
well having Joel just sit there in
silence while young women blame him for
stuff that wasn't his fault is one of
the hallmarks of the second game So I
guess there's no reason not to shove it
in here too." Like I say little
decisions like these do help to correct
the mistakes made by the game
Unfortunately they come with their own
sets of problems Given Mini Gigantor a
clear motivation right off the bat and
drawing out Joel's death means that the
shock factor is gone this time around
Basically everyone who's been following
the show knew exactly what it was
leading to and so there's no element of
surprise to his death now It's just
something we all knew was coming
Likewise having a realistically
proportioned actress play Abby makes her
a much more believable and vulnerable
character now which definitely helps the
audience empathize with her The downside
is that Caitlyn Ever is only like 5'2
and looks like she weighs about 100 lb
soaking wet So how can I put this She's
not the most intimidating antagonist
you'll ever see In fact most of her gang
absolutely tower over her So it's kind
of hard to buy her as this hard driving
intimidating leader that they'll follow
to the ends of the earth The bigger
question though is where exactly does
the show go from here without Joel to
anchor it Much as Pedro Pascal has
become a double-barreled meme for his
habit of showing up in absolutely
everything and being possibly the worst
dressed man in all of Hollywood one
thing you can't deny is that he's a
pretty good actor who brings a certain
charisma to most projects he's in
Without him around it's really up to
Bella Ramsay to carry the show now
And well I think you can see where I'm
going with this one Her version of Ellie
is nowhere near as likable and
compelling as her game counterpart And
with nobody to play against and balance
her out it does make me wonder if the
worst thing about this show will end up
being its [ __ ] protagonists I don't
know man Up until now we've wondered if
The Last of Us was going to follow in
the footsteps of the video games or
course correct in response to all the
criticism It's currently doing a bit of
both but definitely leaning towards the
former Time will tell if the gamble pays
off Also just a little reminder that
there's about 10 days left to go on the
Trial by Fire Kickstarter We're well
past our initial funding goal now so
we're pushing really hard in these last
couple of weeks to reach our stretch
goals so we can produce more and
hopefully more ambitious and complex
short films So if you'd like to support
independent film projects and make all
our creative dreams come true the link
is in the
description Anyway that's all I've got
for
today Go away now
